title: SenderIdOrSelf validates Id at deserialization time, rejecting empty strings
description: SenderIdOrSelf's Deserialize impl calls Id::new(s) for non-'self' values, which rejects empty strings at the serde boundary via map_err(serde::de::Error::custom). This means a malformed server payload with senderId: '' fails deserialization rather than producing a Contact(Id) with an empty inner string. A contributor might suggest removing the validation and using Id::from_trusted() for 'simplicity', or deferring validation to call sites. Both approaches allow invalid data to enter the type system and propagate. Rejecting at the deserialization boundary is the correct place because it is the only point where server-supplied untrusted input crosses into the application's type-safe domain.
